source.title: CPIC Guideline Update for TPMT and NUDT15 Genotypes and Dosing of Thiopurines
source.finding: NUDT15 *3 homozygous: consider alternative to thiopurines. Common in Asian populations.

TT.effect: Complete NUDT15 deficiency. Very high risk of life-threatening myelosuppression with thiopurines. Especially prevalent in East Asian populations (~2%).
TT.recommendation: Avoid thiopurines or use <10% of standard dose. Consider alternative immunosuppressant.

CT.effect: Reduced NUDT15 activity. Increased risk of leukopenia with thiopurines.
CT.recommendation: Reduce thiopurine dose to 25-50% of standard. Monitor CBC weekly at start.

CC.recommendation: Standard thiopurine dosing with routine monitoring.
